instruction: Read each passage carefully and answer the questions that follow. Answers must be based only on the given passage.

passages.passage: Griffin was a brilliant scientist who discovered how to make himself invisible. He carried out experiments by swallowing certain rare drugs which made his body transparent. However, instead of using his discovery for the benefit of humanity, he misused it for selfish purposes.
questions: Who was Griffin?
What special discovery did Griffin make?
How did Griffin achieve invisibility?
Was Griffin’s discovery used for humanity?
For what purpose did Griffin misuse his power?

passages.passage: After becoming invisible, Griffin set fire to his landlord’s house because he was angry. His landlord had disturbed him and demanded rent. This act showed Griffin’s violent and lawless nature.
questions: What did Griffin do to his landlord’s house?
Why did Griffin set the house on fire?
Who demanded rent from Griffin?
What does this act reveal about Griffin?
Which quality of Griffin is highlighted in this passage?

passages.passage: Griffin was forced to wander naked because clothes did not become invisible. Winter was the worst season for him as he could not protect himself from the cold. Hunger and homelessness made his life miserable.
questions: Why was Griffin forced to wander naked?
Which season troubled Griffin the most?
Why was winter dangerous for him?
What problems did Griffin face while wandering?
How was Griffin’s life affected after invisibility?

passages.passage: One day, people noticed footprints appearing on the ground without seeing anyone. The mystery frightened them. The footprints were visible because Griffin’s feet were muddy even though his body was invisible.
questions: What strange sight did people notice?
Why did the footprints create fear?
Who caused the footprints?
Why were the footprints visible?
What condition of Griffin caused the footprints?

passages.passage: To survive, Griffin hid in a large departmental store in London. He stole food, clothes, and wine. However, he slept too long and was discovered when the shop opened in the morning.
questions: Where did Griffin hide to survive?
What items did Griffin steal from the store?
Why did Griffin hide in the store?
What mistake did Griffin make?
When was Griffin discovered?

passages.passage: Griffin went to the village of Iping to live in solitude. He stayed at an inn run by Mrs Hall. He always kept his face covered and avoided people, which made the villagers suspicious.
questions: Why did Griffin go to Iping?
Where did Griffin stay in Iping?
Who ran the inn where Griffin stayed?
How did Griffin behave around people?
What effect did his behaviour have on villagers?

passages.passage: Strange things began to happen at the inn. Furniture moved on its own and doors opened without anyone touching them. These events made people believe the place was haunted.
questions: What strange events occurred at the inn?
What moved without human touch?
How did doors behave strangely?
What did people believe about the inn?
Why were people frightened by these events?

passages.passage: The villagers suspected Griffin when he suddenly produced money after claiming he had none. His secrecy and strange behaviour increased their doubts about him.
questions: Why did villagers suspect Griffin?
What had Griffin earlier claimed about money?
What change increased suspicion?
Which behaviour made Griffin doubtful?
Who became suspicious of Griffin?

passages.passage: When questioned, Griffin removed his bandages and clothes. People saw a headless man standing before them. This shocking sight terrified everyone present.
questions: What did Griffin remove during the confrontation?
What shocking sight did people see?
Why did people feel terrified?
How did Griffin reveal his invisibility?
What reaction did the revelation cause?

passages.passage: Griffin escaped easily as he was completely invisible. His misuse of science made him a danger to society. The story teaches that power without responsibility leads to destruction.
questions: How did Griffin manage to escape?
Why was Griffin dangerous to society?
What did Griffin misuse?
What lesson does the story teach?
What happens when power lacks responsibility?
